title: Internal Citation Style Rules (APA 7th Edition)
body:
## APA 7th Edition Citation Style Guidelines

### General Format
- Author(s) last name, followed by initials. Multiple authors: separate with commas and an ampersand (&) before the last author.
- Publication year in parentheses, followed by a period.
- Title of the work: italicize titles of standalone works (books, journals, reports); do not italicize titles of articles or chapters. Capitalize only the first word of the title and subtitle, and proper nouns.
- Source information: for articles, include journal name (italicized), volume number (italicized), issue number in parentheses (not italicized), and page range. End with DOI or URL if available.
- For web pages: author, date, title (italicized), site name, URL.

### In-Text Citations
- Use author-date format: (Smith, 2021) or Smith (2021) for narrative citations.
- For multiple authors: two authors always cite both; three or more cite first author followed by 'et al.' (e.g., Chen et al., 2020).

### Reference List
- Hanging indent: first line flush left, subsequent lines indented 0.5 inches.
- Order: alphabetical by first author's surname.
- DOIs: present as hyperlinks (https://doi.org/...). Do not add a period after the DOI.

These rules apply strictly to all internal knowledge base reference articles.
